Note: (8 bit) - Debug mode screen and Nickjones screen = 6b, other time = 00
(8 bit) - during Nick Jones cheat goes from 00 to 03. Other time = 00

[8-bit] [Binary] Debug Menu Indicator

0x00 = Never used
0x01 = Used

Persists even through game overs/continues

[16-bit] Map View Mode Cheat

0x0000 = Off
0xffff = On

This is from the debug menu

[16-bit] Cheat Code Input

0x0000 = On pause screen or code error reset
0xffff = Cheat code entered

0x0007 -> 0xffff (1 frame) -> 0x0000 = Incorrect code
0x0007 -> 0xffff (persists more than 1 frame) -> 0x0000 = Correct code

This increments by 0x01 to 0x07 per input (even incorrect ones). This also flickers rapidly between 0x0000 and 0xffff every 3 seconds (approximately) of inactivity which resets the counter (so players have approximately 3 seconds between presses to enter cheats)

[Array] [16 bytes] [16-bit] Pause Inputs (For Cheats)

0x0010 = R
0x0020 = L
0x0040 = X
0x0080 = 
0x0100 = Right
0x0200 = Left
0x0400 = Down
0x0800 = Up
0x2000 = Select
0x4000 = Y
0x8000 = B

Button combinations are additive. These are clearly bitflags for button presses, but they are specifically tied to the Pause menu to register cheats Will reset after approximately 3 seconds of inactivity between presses

[8-bit] Current Level

0x00 = New Junk City
0x01 = What the Heck (first)
0x02 = What the Heck (snowman boss)
0x03 = What the Heck (evil cat)
0x04 = Bungee 1
0x05 = Bungee 2
0x06 = Bungee 3
0x07 = Peter Puppy
0x08 = Slug for Butt
0x09 = Helicopter
0x0a = Prof's Lab
0x0b = Prof's Lab 2
0x0c = Naked Worm
0x0d = Sea Tunnel
0x0e = Pod Race
0x0f = Asteroids 1
0x10 = Asteroids 2

0x12 = Asteroids 4
0x13 = Asteroids 5
0x14 = Asteroids 6
0x15 = Darkness 1
0x16 = Darkness 2
0x17 = Darkness 3

[16-bit] Freezability Cheat

0x0000 = Off
0xffff = On

This is from the debug menu

[16-bit] Cheat Mode

0x0000 = Not on
0xffff = On

This is from the debug menu

(8 bit) - value corresponds to levels in a game:
level 1 = d9

(8 bit) - value corresponds to levels in a game:
level 1 = 39

[16-bit] Demo Mode Indicator

0x0000 = In-game
0xffff = Demo Mode (or reset to main menu)

Demo mode persists until player engages

[8 bit] Jim Animation 

0x21 = Jim hits spikes
0x25 = Jim loses Andy Asteroids
0x33 = Jim blasts off at end of standard level
0x37 = Jim wins Andy's Asteroids

[16-bit] Animation ID

0x1707 = Jim idles
0x18bf = Jim has blasted his own face
0x1902 = Jim twirls guns and tosses it
0x19f3 = Jim flexes
0x1a05 = Jim flexes too hard
0x1a7f = Jim loses his pants
0x1b37 = Jim jumps rope

Related with levels again (15 = continue)
(8 bit) = value goes to 04 in buttvile during start of miniboss fight

(8 bit) - 2e - beginning of the level (text before the level start)
0xea=Psycrow Won!
(8 bit) - 1e - beginning of the level (text before the level start)
(8 bit) - text on the screen (including "Cow Launched") = 8a

(8 bit) - Health of mini boss in Buttville
on difficult maximum health = 1e
(perhaps it could spawn in different address sometimes?)
Boss 1 Health (and J Maggot boss on normal)
Number of times that was hit in the sea pod - Slot 2
16 Bit = ffff - Bathyscaphe uses slot 2 HP
(8 bit) - in the first level value 07 = Billy the Bin is dead
Number of times that was hit in the sea pod - Slot 1
16 Bit = ffff - Bathyscaphe uses slot 1 HP

[Array] [4 bytes] [Lower4] Basic Gun Ammo

Upper4 is always 0x3

Byte 1: Thousands digit
Byte 2: Hundreds digit
Byte 3: Tens digit
Byte 4: Ones digit

[Array] [4 bytes] [Lower4] Health

Upper4 is always 0x3

Byte 1: Thousands digit
Byte 2: Hundreds digit
Byte 3: Tens digit
Byte 4: Ones digit
